title: andropogon × paraguariensis
intro: Andropogon × paraguariensis, also known as Yerba Mate, is a hybrid plant species derived from the combination of the species Stenotaphrum secundatum (St. August grass) and Andropogon bicornis (a species of grass). Yerba Mate has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The health benefits of Yerba Mate include improved digestion, boosted energy, and enhanced mental clarity, making it a popular ingredient in herbal preparations such as teas and infusions. Yerba Mate is commonly consumed as a traditional South American drink, known as Mate, which is made by steeping the leaves in hot water, and can also be found in supplement form.
